    5                                                module rdi_router (
    6                                                
    7                                                    input  logic         rst_n,
    8                                                    input  logic         reset,       // Sideband or link reset flag
    9                                                    
    10                                                   // Inputs from Arbiter (RDI TX path)
    11                                                   input  logic [127:0] rdi_msg,
    12                                                   input  logic         rdi_vld,
    13                                                   output logic         rdi_rdy,
    14                                                   
    15                                                   // Outputs to Register Access Block
    16                                                   output logic [127:0] reg_msg,
    17                                                   output logic         reg_vld,
    18                                                   input  logic         reg_rdy,
    19                                                   
    20                                                   // Outputs to Link (Adapter down-to-Link)
    21                                                   output logic [127:0] adapter_msg_send,
    22                                                   output logic         adapter_vld_send,
    23                                                   input  logic         adapter_rdy
    24                                               );
    25                                                   
    26                                                   sb_opcode_e   opcode;
    27                                                   sb_dstid_e    dstid;
    28                                                   logic         is_req;
    29                                                   logic         is_local_phy;
    30                                                   logic         consumer_rdy;
    31                                                   
    32              1                          2         assign opcode       = sb_opcode_e'(rdi_msg[4:0]);
    33              1                          2         assign dstid        = sb_dstid_e'(rdi_msg[58:56]);
    34                                                   
    35                                                   // opcode[4] == 0 means it's a request (MEM, CFG, DMS Read/Write)
    36                                                   // opcode[4] == 1 means it's a message or completion
    37                                                   assign is_req       = ~rdi_msg[4]; 
    38                                                   
    39              1                          2         assign is_local_phy = (dstid == LOCAL_PHY);
    40                                               
    41                                                   // Buffer accepts data if it's empty or being consumed in the current cycle
    42                                                   assign rdi_rdy = consumer_rdy;
    43                                               
    44                                                   // Routing Logic based on reset flag and message fields
    45              1                          8         always_comb begin
    46                                                       // Defaults
    47              1                          8             reg_vld          = 1'b0;
    48              1                          8             adapter_vld_send = 1'b0;
    49              1                          8             consumer_rdy   = 1'b0;
    50                                                       
    51              1                          8             reg_msg          = rdi_msg;
    52              1                          8             adapter_msg_send = rdi_msg;
    53                                               
    54                                                       
    55                                                       if (reset) begin
    56                                                           // In reset mode
    57                                                           if (is_req) begin
    58                                                               // Route all requests (local & remote) to Register Access Block
    59                                                               // so it can generate a completion and return it.
    60              1                          1                     reg_vld        = rdi_vld;
    61              1                          1                     consumer_rdy = reg_rdy;
    62                                                           end else begin
    63                                                               // Drop normal messages / completions without sending to link
    64                                                               // Assert consumer_rdy to pop it from buffer, freeing up space.
    65              1                          2                     consumer_rdy   = 1'b1;
    66              1                          2                     adapter_vld_send = 1'b0;
    67                                                           end
    68                                                       end else begin
    69                                                           // Normal mode
    70                                                           if (is_local_phy) begin
    71              1                    ***0***                     reg_vld        = rdi_vld;
    72              1                    ***0***                     consumer_rdy = reg_rdy;
    73                                                           end else begin
    74              1                          5                     adapter_vld_send = rdi_vld;
    75              1                          5                     consumer_rdy   = adapter_rdy;

  File rtl/SideBand/common/sb_priority_arbiter.sv
    2                                                module sb_priority_arbiter #(
    3                                                    parameter int DATA_WIDTH = 128
    4                                                ) (
    5                                                    // High Priority Input Interface
    6                                                    input  logic [DATA_WIDTH-1:0] hip_msg,
    7                                                    input  logic                  hip_vld,
    8                                                    output logic                  hip_rdy,
    9                                                
    10                                                   // Low Priority Input Interface
    11                                                   input  logic [DATA_WIDTH-1:0] lop_msg,
    12                                                   input  logic                  lop_vld,
    13                                                   output logic                  lop_rdy,
    14                                               
    15                                                   // Arbitrated Output Interface
    16                                                   output logic [DATA_WIDTH-1:0] out_msg,
    17                                                   output logic                  out_vld,
    18                                                   input  logic                  out_rdy
    19                                               );
    20                                                   sb_packet_t sb_packet;
    21              1                          7         always_comb begin
    22                                                       // Priority 1: High Priority Input
    23                                                       if (hip_vld) begin
    24              1                          1                 out_msg = hip_msg;
    25              1                          1                 out_vld = 1'b1;
    26                                                       end
    27                                                       // Priority 2: Low Priority Input
    28                                                       else begin
    29              1                          6                 out_msg = lop_msg;
    30              1                          6                 out_vld = lop_vld;
    31                                                       end
    32                                               
    33                                                       // ── Ready signals ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    34                                                       // rdy MUST be independent of vld (standard valid/ready handshake).
    35                                                       // hip_rdy: downstream is free (out_rdy=1) and no starvation.
    36                                                       // lop_rdy: downstream is free AND no high-priority msg is present.
    37              1                          7             hip_rdy = out_rdy;
    38              1                          7             lop_rdy = out_rdy && !hip_vld;

    Line         Item                      Count     Source 
    ----         ----                      -----     ------ 
  File rtl/common/FIFO/fifo_wptr_full.sv
    12                                               module fifo_wptr_full #(
    13                                                   parameter int  ADDR_WIDTH = 4
    14                                               ) (
    15                                                   input  logic                  wclk,
    16                                                   input  logic                  wrst_n,
    17                                                   input  logic                  winc,
    18                                                   input  logic [ADDR_WIDTH:0]   wq2_rptr,   // synced read pointer (Gray if ASYNC, Binary if SYNC)
    19                                                   output logic                  wfull,
    20                                                   output logic [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] waddr,
    21                                                   output logic [ADDR_WIDTH:0]   wptr_gray   // Gray-coded write pointer (used only in ASYNC mode)
    22                                               );
    23                                               
    24                                                   logic [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wptr;          // Binary write pointer
    25                                                   logic [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wptr_gray_comb;
    26                                               
    27                                                   // Binary → Gray conversion
    28              1                          3         assign wptr_gray_comb = (wptr >> 1) ^ wptr;
    29              1                          3         assign waddr          = wptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0];
    30                                               
    31                                                   // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    32                                                   // Full flag
    33                                                   //   ASYNC : compare ~Gray(wptr)[MSBs] vs synced Gray(rptr)  [2-FF sync]
    34                                                   //   SYNC  : same comparison, wq2_rptr = Gray(rptr) from same domain
    35                                                   //   The top two bits of the next Gray-coded write pointer are inverted
    36                                                   //   vs. the wrapped read pointer when the FIFO is full.
    37                                                   // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    38              1                          5         assign wfull = ({~wptr_gray_comb[ADDR_WIDTH : ADDR_WIDTH-1],
    39                                                                     wptr_gray_comb[ADDR_WIDTH-2 : 0]} == wq2_rptr);
    40                                               
    41                                                   // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    42                                                   // Write pointer register
    43                                                   // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    44              1                          5         always_ff @(posedge wclk or negedge wrst_n) begin
    45                                                       if (!wrst_n)
    46              1                          2                 wptr <= '0;
    47                                                       else if (winc && !wfull)
    48              1                          1                 wptr <= wptr + 1'b1;
    49                                                   end
    50                                               
    51                                                   // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    52                                                   // Gray-coded write pointer register (used by synchronizer in ASYNC mode)
    53                                                   // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    54              1                          5         always_ff @(posedge wclk or negedge wrst_n) begin
    55                                                       if (!wrst_n)
    56              1                          2                 wptr_gray <= '0;
    57                                                       else
    58              1                          3                 wptr_gray <= wptr_gray_comb;

    12                                               module fifo_wptr_full #(
    13                                                   parameter int  ADDR_WIDTH = 4
    14                                               ) (
    15                                                   input  logic                  wclk,
    16                                                   input  logic                  wrst_n,
    17                                                   input  logic                  winc,
    18                                                   input  logic [ADDR_WIDTH:0]   wq2_rptr,   // synced read pointer (Gray if ASYNC, Binary if SYNC)
    19                                                   output logic                  wfull,
    20                                                   output logic [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] waddr,
    21                                                   output logic [ADDR_WIDTH:0]   wptr_gray   // Gray-coded write pointer (used only in ASYNC mode)
    22                                               );
    23                                               
    24                                                   logic [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wptr;          // Binary write pointer
    25                                                   logic [ADDR_WIDTH:0] wptr_gray_comb;
    26                                               
    27                                                   // Binary → Gray conversion
    28              1                          2         assign wptr_gray_comb = (wptr >> 1) ^ wptr;
    29              1                          2         assign waddr          = wptr[ADDR_WIDTH-1:0];
    30                                               
    31                                                   // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    32                                                   // Full flag
    33                                                   //   ASYNC : compare ~Gray(wptr)[MSBs] vs synced Gray(rptr)  [2-FF sync]
    34                                                   //   SYNC  : same comparison, wq2_rptr = Gray(rptr) from same domain
    35                                                   //   The top two bits of the next Gray-coded write pointer are inverted
    36                                                   //   vs. the wrapped read pointer when the FIFO is full.
    37                                                   // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    38              1                          3         assign wfull = ({~wptr_gray_comb[ADDR_WIDTH : ADDR_WIDTH-1],
    39                                                                     wptr_gray_comb[ADDR_WIDTH-2 : 0]} == wq2_rptr);
    40                                               
    41                                                   // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    42                                                   // Write pointer register
    43                                                   // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    44              1                          3         always_ff @(posedge wclk or negedge wrst_n) begin
    45                                                       if (!wrst_n)
    46              1                          2                 wptr <= '0;
    47                                                       else if (winc && !wfull)
    48              1                    ***0***                 wptr <= wptr + 1'b1;
    49                                                   end
    50                                               
    51                                                   // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    52                                                   // Gray-coded write pointer register (used by synchronizer in ASYNC mode)
    53                                                   // ------------------------------------------------------------------
    54              1                          3         always_ff @(posedge wclk or negedge wrst_n) begin
    55                                                       if (!wrst_n)
    56              1                          2                 wptr_gray <= '0;
    57                                                       else
    58              1                          1                 wptr_gray <= wptr_gray_comb;

================================Statement Details================================

Statement Coverage for instance /SideBand_Top_tb/dut_inst[1]/dut/u_rdi_control/u_credit_counter --

    Line         Item                      Count     Source 
    ----         ----                      -----     ------ 
  File rtl/SideBand/rdi_controller/credit_counter.sv
    25                                               module credit_counter #(
    26                                                   parameter int CRD_W    = 5,          // counter width  (max = 2^CRD_W - 1)
    27                                                   parameter int CRD_INIT = 4           // credits at reset
    28                                               )(
    29                                                   input  logic clk,
    30                                                   input  logic rst_n,
    31                                               
    32                                                   input  logic crd_in,        // +1 : credit received from peer (lp_cfg_crd)
    33                                                   input  logic crd_out,       // -1 : credit consumed (request forwarded)
    34                                               
    35                                                   output logic no_crd         // 1 when count == 0 (gate requests)
    36                                               );
    37                                               
    38                                                   logic [CRD_W-1:0] count;
    39                                               
    40              1                          5         always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    41                                                       if (!rst_n) begin
    42              1                          2                 count <= CRD_W'(CRD_INIT-1);
    43                                                       end else begin
    44                                                           case ({crd_in, crd_out})
    45                                                               // inc only – guard against overflow
    46              1                          1                     2'b10 : count <= (count == '1)        ? count : count + 1'b1;
    47                                                               // dec only – guard against underflow
    48              1                    ***0***                     2'b01 : count <= (count == '0)        ? count : count - 1'b1;
    49                                                               // both simultaneously – net zero
    50              1                          2                     2'b11 : count <= count;
    51                                                               // no change
    52              1                    ***0***                     default: count <= count;
    53                                                           endcase
    54                                                       end
    55                                                   end
    56                                               
    57              1                          2         assign no_crd = (count == '0);

    2                                                module sb_priority_arbiter #(
    3                                                    parameter int DATA_WIDTH = 128
    4                                                ) (
    5                                                    // High Priority Input Interface
    6                                                    input  logic [DATA_WIDTH-1:0] hip_msg,
    7                                                    input  logic                  hip_vld,
    8                                                    output logic                  hip_rdy,
    9                                                
    10                                                   // Low Priority Input Interface
    11                                                   input  logic [DATA_WIDTH-1:0] lop_msg,
    12                                                   input  logic                  lop_vld,
    13                                                   output logic                  lop_rdy,
    14                                               
    15                                                   // Arbitrated Output Interface
    16                                                   output logic [DATA_WIDTH-1:0] out_msg,
    17                                                   output logic                  out_vld,
    18                                                   input  logic                  out_rdy
    19                                               );
    20                                                   sb_packet_t sb_packet;
    21              1                          6         always_comb begin
    22                                                       // Priority 1: High Priority Input
    23                                                       if (hip_vld) begin
    24              1                    ***0***                 out_msg = hip_msg;
    25              1                    ***0***                 out_vld = 1'b1;
    26                                                       end
    27                                                       // Priority 2: Low Priority Input
    28                                                       else begin
    29              1                          6                 out_msg = lop_msg;
    30              1                          6                 out_vld = lop_vld;
    31                                                       end
    32                                               
    33                                                       // ── Ready signals ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    34                                                       // rdy MUST be independent of vld (standard valid/ready handshake).
    35                                                       // hip_rdy: downstream is free (out_rdy=1) and no starvation.
    36                                                       // lop_rdy: downstream is free AND no high-priority msg is present.
    37              1                          6             hip_rdy = out_rdy;
    38              1                          6             lop_rdy = out_rdy && !hip_vld;

    136                                              
    137             1                          6         assign pl_msg_rdy = (state == IDLE) || (state == OUTPUT_STATE && chunk_cnt == expected_chunks);
    138             1                          5         assign traffic_req  = (state == WAIT_READY);
    139                                              
    140                                              
    141                                                  ////////////////////////////////////////
    142                                                  // datapath
    143                                                  ////////////////////////////////////////
    144                                              
    145             1                          9         always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    146                                              
    147                                                      if(!rst_n) begin
    148             1                          2                 msg_reg         <= '0;
    149             1                          2                 chunk_cnt       <= 0;
    150             1                          2                 expected_chunks <= 0;
    151             1                          2                 pl_cfg_vld      <= 0;
    152             1                          2                 pl_cfg          <= 0;
    153                                                      end
    154                                              
    155                                                      else begin
    156                                              
    157                                                          case(state)
    158                                              
    159                                                          ///////////////////////////////////////
    160                                                          IDLE:
    161                                                          ///////////////////////////////////////
    162                                                          begin
    163                                                              if(pl_msg_vld) begin
    164             1                          1                         msg_reg         <= msg;
    165             1                          1                         expected_chunks <= next_expected_chunks;
    166                                                              end
    167             1                          4                     pl_cfg_vld <= 0;
    168             1                          4                     chunk_cnt  <= 0;
    169                                                          end
    170                                              
    171                                                          ///////////////////////////////////////
    172                                                          WAIT_READY:
    173                                                          ///////////////////////////////////////
    174                                                          begin
    175                                                              if (traffic_rdy) begin
    176             1                          1                         chunk_cnt  <= 1;
    177             1                          1                         pl_cfg_vld <= 1'b1;
    178                                                                  // Send out the first chunk
    179             1                          1                         pl_cfg     <= msg_reg[31:0]; 
    180                                                              end
    181                                                              else begin
    182             1                    ***0***                         pl_cfg_vld <= 0;
    183                                                              end
    184                                                          end
    185                                              
    186                                                          ///////////////////////////////////////
    187                                                          OUTPUT_STATE:
    188                                                          ///////////////////////////////////////
    189                                                          begin
    190                                                              if(chunk_cnt == expected_chunks) begin
    191                                                                  // Last chunk was alrdy sent out in the previous cycle
    192                                                                  if(pl_msg_vld) begin
    193                                                                      // Back to back consecutive packets: buffer and wait for rdy
    194             1                    ***0***                             msg_reg         <= msg;
    195             1                    ***0***                             expected_chunks <= next_expected_chunks;
    196                                                                  end
    197             1                          1                         pl_cfg_vld <= 0;
    198             1                          1                         chunk_cnt  <= 0;
    199                                                              end
    200                                                              else begin
    201             1                          1                         pl_cfg_vld <= 1'b1;
    202             1                          1                         chunk_cnt  <= chunk_cnt + 1;
    203                                                                  case(chunk_cnt)
    204             1                          1                             1: pl_cfg <= msg_flat[63:32];
    205             1                    ***0***                             2: pl_cfg <= msg_flat[95:64];
    206             1                    ***0***                             3: pl_cfg <= msg_flat[127:96];
    207             1                    ***0***                             default: pl_cfg <= msg_flat[31:0];

================================Statement Details================================

Statement Coverage for instance /SideBand_Top_tb/dut_inst[1]/dut/u_reg_access/u_depacketizer --

    Line         Item                      Count     Source 
    ----         ----                      -----     ------ 
  File rtl/SideBand/Reg_Access/Reg_DePacketizer.sv
    37                                               module Reg_DePacketizer
    38                                                   import sb_pkg::*;
    39                                               (
    40                                                   input  logic         clk,
    41                                                   input  logic         rst_n,
    42                                               
    43                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    44                                                   // Incoming SB request packet (latched by whoever feeds this block)
    45                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    46                                                   input  logic [127:0] reg_msg,     // Full 128-bit SB packet
    47                                                   input  logic         reg_vld,    // Packet is valid / latched
    48                                                   input  logic         reg_rdy,    // Rdy to accept new request
    49                                               
    50                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    51                                                   // Control outputs → Reg_Access_FSM
    52                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    53                                                   output sb_opcode_e   opcode,        // [4:0]  from header[4:0]
    54                                                   output logic         parity_err,    // 1 when control-parity fails
    55                                                   output logic         ep,            // Error Poison bit (header[63])
    56                                                   output logic         false_msg,     // 1 when dstid is not a reg-access target
    57                                               
    58                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    59                                                   // Datapath outputs → Reg_File
    60                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    61                                                   output logic [24:0]  rf_addr,       // {space, RL[3:0], offset[19:0]}
    62                                                   output logic [7:0]   rf_be,         // Byte enables  (from header[21:14])
    63                                                   output logic         rf_is_64b_access,  // 1 for 64-bit opcodes
    64                                                   output logic [63:0]  rf_wdata,      // Write payload (reg_msg[127:64])
    65                                               
    66                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    67                                                   // Raw header latch → Completion_gen
    68                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    69                                                   output logic [63:0]  Original_Header   // header[63:0], latched at reg_vld
    70                                               );
    71                                               
    72                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    73                                               // Internal Latch for the Packet
    74                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75                                               logic [127:0] latched_pkt;
    76                                               
    77              1                          3     always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    78                                                   if (!rst_n)
    79              1                          2             latched_pkt <= '0;
    80                                                   else if (reg_vld && reg_rdy)
    81              1                    ***0***             latched_pkt <= reg_msg;
    82                                               end
    83                                               
    84                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    85                                               // Unpack header fields from the latched packet
    86                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    87                                               sb_header_u hdr;
    88              1                          2     assign hdr = sb_header_u'(latched_pkt[63:0]);   // Header occupies lower 64 bits
    89                                               
    90                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    91                                               // Always-comb outputs – decoded purely from latched packet
    92                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    93              1                          2     always_comb begin
    94                                                   // ── Control path ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    95              1                          2         opcode          = hdr.req.opcode;
    96              1                          2         ep              = hdr.req.ep;       // Error Poison from request header bit 5
    97                                               
    98                                                   // Control parity check: even parity over header[62:0]
    99                                                   // cp is stored in hdr.req.cp (bit[62]); parity of hdr[62:0] should == hdr.cp
    100                                                  // even parity: ^(hdr[62:0]) == 1 means even number of 1s → bit should be 1
    101                                                  // The standard check: cp_expected = (^hdr[61:0]); error if cp_expected != hdr.cp
    102             1                          2         parity_err      = (hdr.req.cp != (^(latched_pkt[61:0])));
    103                                              
    104                                                  // false_msg: dstid is not a register-access destination
    105                                                  // According to UCIe spec, reg-access packets are addressed to LOCAL_ADAPTER(001),
    106                                                  // LOCAL_PHY(010), or REMOTE_REG_ACCESS(100).  Any other dstid is a false message.
    107             1                          2         false_msg       = (hdr.req.dstid != LOCAL_PHY);
    108                                              
    109                                                  // ── Address decode ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    110                                                  // space bit: CFG opcodes → 0, MEM/DMS opcodes → 1
    111                                                  // MsgInfo [55:40] layout per spec:
    112                                                  //   [55:52] = RL[3:0]  (Register Locator, 4 bits)
    113                                                  //   [51:40] = addr[19:8]   \  12-bit DWORD offset
    114                                                  // But looking at UCIe spec §9 table, the 20-bit byte offset is encoded as:
    115                                                  //   MsgInfo[55:40] = {RL[3:0], offset[19:4]}   (offset in units of 16B? No...)
    116                                                  // The spec §3 says the "address" field for reg-access is a DWORD offset:
    117                                                  //   MsgInfo[55:40] → Top 16 bits: {RL[3:0], addr[11:0]}  where addr is DWORD offset
    118                                                  //   The full 20-bit byte offset = {addr_dword[9:0], 2'b00}   (×4 for DWORD)
    119                                                  // However, since Reg_File expects a byte offset [19:0], we simply mirror the
    120                                                  // 16 bits from MsgInfo and pad with 4 zeros to reach the needed resolution:
    121                                                  //   rf_addr[19:0] = {MsgInfo[11:0], 4'b0000}   (12-bit DWORD offset → 16-bit byte, padded to 20)
    122                                                  // For clarity we use: {RL[3:0]=MsgInfo[15:12], byteOffset[19:0]=MsgInfo[11:0]<<4 + BE lsb}
    123                                                  //
    124                                                  // Simplified practical construction (aligned with what Reg_File uses §9 offsets):
    125                                                  //   space  [24]    = opcode[3] (bit 3 selects MEM vs CFG)
    126                                                  //   RL     [23:20] = hdr.MsgInfo[15:12]  (upper 4 bits of MsgInfo's address field)
    127                                                  //   offset [19:0]  = {hdr.MsgInfo[11:0], hdr.msgcode[7:4]} (12b DWORD offset + sub-DWORD from BE top nibble)
    128                                                  //
    129                                                  // Simplest and most consistent with UCIe §3.5 "Address" field description:
    130                                                  //   The 16-bit MsgInfo[15:0] = {RL[3:0], DWORD_offset[11:0]}
    131                                                  //   byte_offset = DWORD_offset << 2  →  14 bits, zero-padded to 20
    132                                              
    133             1                          2         rf_addr[24]    = (opcode == SB_32_CFG_READ ||
    134                                                                    opcode == SB_32_CFG_WRITE||
    135                                                                    opcode == SB_64_CFG_READ ||
    136                                                                    opcode == SB_64_CFG_WRITE) ? 1'b0 : 1'b1;           // bit3 of opcode: 0=CFG, 1=MEM
    137             1                          2         rf_addr[23:0]  = hdr.req.addr;       // Directly use the 24-bit address
    138                                              
    139                                                  // ── Byte enables ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    140                                                  // Spec §3.4: BE[7:0] are carried in header bits [19:12] = be field
    141             1                          2         rf_be           = hdr.req.be;         // Direct access from struct
    142                                                  // ── 64-bit access flag ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    143             1                          2         rf_is_64b_access = opcode[3];         // 64-bit opcodes have opcode[3]=1
    144                                              
    145                                                  // ── Write data ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    146             1                          2         rf_wdata        = latched_pkt[127:64];     // Payload = upper 64 bits of packet
    147                                              end
    148                                              
    149                                              // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    150                                              // Original_Header latch (registered at packet-valid)
    151                                              // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    152             1                          2     assign Original_Header = latched_pkt[63:0];

Statement Coverage for instance /SideBand_Top_tb/dut_inst[1]/dut/u_reg_access/u_fsm --

    Line         Item                      Count     Source 
    ----         ----                      -----     ------ 
  File rtl/SideBand/Reg_Access/Reg_Access_FSM.sv
    7                                                module Reg_Access_FSM
    8                                                    import sb_pkg::*; 
    9                                                (
    10                                                   input  logic         clk,
    11                                                   input  logic         rst_n,
    12                                               
    13                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    14                                                   // Handshake with Top Level / RDI_CONTROL
    15                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    16                                                   input  logic         reg_vld,        // Valid request from RDI
    17                                                   output logic         reg_rdy,        // Rdy to accept new request
    18                                                   input  logic         completion_rdy, // TX is rdy to accept completion
    19                                               
    20                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    21                                                   // From Reg_DePacketizer (Control Path Only)
    22                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    23                                                   input  sb_opcode_e   opcode,         // [4:0] Opcode
    24                                                   input  logic         parity_err,
    25                                                   input  logic         ep,             // Error Poison
    26                                                   input  logic         false_msg,
    27                                               
    28                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    29                                                   // To / From Register File (RF)
    30                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    31                                                   output logic         rd_en,          // Read enable
    32                                                   output logic         wr_en,          // Write enable
    33                                                   input  logic         rdata_vld,      // Register file read-data rdy
    34                                                   input  logic         rf_addr_err,
    35                                               
    36                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    37                                                   // To Completion_gen
    38                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    39                                                   output logic [2:0]   status,         // 3'b000=SC, 3'b001=UR
    40                                                   output logic         completion_start
    41                                               );
    42                                               
    43                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    44                                               // State encoding
    45                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    46                                               typedef enum logic [1:0] {
    47                                                   IDLE     = 2'b00,
    48                                                   DECODE   = 2'b01,
    49                                                   EXECUTE  = 2'b10,
    50                                                   GEN      = 2'b11
    51                                               } state_t;
    52                                               
    53                                               state_t current_state, next_state;
    54                                               
    55                                               // Internal signals
    56                                               logic is_read;
    57                                               logic error;
    58                                               
    59                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    60                                               // Opcode classification
    61                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    62              1                          2     always_comb begin
    63              1                          2         is_read = (opcode == SB_32_MEM_READ) || (opcode == SB_32_CFG_READ) || 
    64                                                             (opcode == SB_64_MEM_READ) || (opcode == SB_64_CFG_READ);
    65                                               end
    66                                               
    67                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    68                                               // Error detection (Added Poison 'ep' flag)
    69                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    70              1                          3     always_comb begin
    71              1                          3         error = parity_err || false_msg || ep || rf_addr_err;
    72                                               end
    73                                               
    74                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75                                               // FSM: State Register
    76                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    77              1                          3     always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    78                                                   if (!rst_n)
    79              1                          2             current_state <= IDLE;
    80                                                   else
    81              1                          1             current_state <= next_state;
    82                                               end
    83                                               
    84                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    85                                               // FSM: Next-State Logic
    86                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    87              1                          6     always_comb begin
    88              1                          6         next_state = current_state; // Default: stay in current state
    89                                               
    90                                                   case (current_state)
    91                                               
    92                                                       IDLE: begin
    93                                                           // Wait for a valid request
    94                                                           if (reg_vld)
    95              1                    ***0***                     next_state = DECODE;
    96                                                       end
    97                                               
    98                                                       DECODE: begin
    99                                                           // Fast-Forward on error to GEN UR response
    100                                                          if (error)
    101             1                    ***0***                     next_state = GEN;
    102                                                          else
    103             1                    ***0***                     next_state = EXECUTE;
    104                                                      end
    105                                              
    106                                                      EXECUTE: begin
    107                                                          // If it's a WRITE (!is_read), exit immediately.
    108                                                          // If it's a READ, wait for rdata_vld.
    109                                                          if (!is_read || rdata_vld)
    110             1                    ***0***                     next_state = GEN;
    111                                                      end
    112                                              
    113                                                      GEN: begin
    114                                                          // Handshake with completion_gen: wait until it's rdy
    115                                                          if (completion_rdy)
    116             1                    ***0***                     next_state = IDLE;
    117                                                      end
    118                                              
    119             1                          2             default: next_state = IDLE;
    120                                              
    121                                                  endcase
    122                                              end
    123                                              
    124                                              // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    125                                              // FSM: Output Logic
    126                                              // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    127             1                          3     always_comb begin
    128                                                  // Default values to prevent unwanted latches
    129             1                          3         reg_rdy          = 1'b0;
    130             1                          3         rd_en            = 1'b0;
    131             1                          3         wr_en            = 1'b0;
    132             1                          3         completion_start = 1'b0;
    133             1                          3         status           = 3'b000;
    134                                              
    135                                                  case (current_state)
    136                                              
    137                                                      IDLE: begin
    138             1                          2                 reg_rdy = 1'b1; // Rdy to receive when idle
    139                                                      end
    140                                              
    141                                                      // DECODE: Outputs remain at default (0)
    142                                              
    143                                                      EXECUTE: begin
    144             1                    ***0***                 rd_en = is_read;
    145             1                    ***0***                 wr_en = !is_read;
    146                                                      end
    147                                              
    148                                                      GEN: begin
    149             1                    ***0***                 completion_start = 1'b1;
    150                                                          // 3'b000 = SC (Success), 3'b001 = UR (Unsupported Request/Error)
    151             1                    ***0***                 status = error ? 3'b001 : 3'b000;

Statement Coverage for instance /SideBand_Top_tb/dut_inst[1]/dut/u_reg_access/u_completion_gen --

    Line         Item                      Count     Source 
    ----         ----                      -----     ------ 
  File rtl/SideBand/Reg_Access/Completion_gen.sv
    12                                               module Completion_gen
    13                                                   import sb_pkg::*;
    14                                               (
    15                                                   input  logic         clk,
    16                                                   input  logic         rst_n,
    17                                               
    18                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    19                                                   // Trigger from FSM
    20                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    21                                                   input  logic         completion_start,  // 1-cycle pulse from FSM:GENERATE
    22                                                   input  logic [2:0]   status,            // SC(000) / UR(001) from FSM
    23                                               
    24                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    25                                                   // From Reg_DePacketizer 
    26                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    27                                                   input  logic[63:0]  Original_Header,   // Latched original request header
    28                                               
    29                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    30                                                   // Read data from Register File
    31                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    32                                                   input  logic[63:0]  rf_rdata,          // Register-file read data
    33                                                   input  logic        rdata_vld,         // Read-data valid
    34                                               
    35                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    36                                                   // Output completion packet to Link_Controller TX path
    37                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    38                                                   output logic[127:0] completion_msg,    // Full 128-bit completion packet
    39                                                   output logic         completion_vld,    // Packet is valid
    40                                                   input  logic         completion_rdy     // Back-pressure from TX arbiter
    41                                               );
    42                                               
    43                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    44                                               // Internal signals
    45                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    46                                               sb_header_u   req_hdr;           // View into original request header
    47                                               sb_header_u   cpl_hdr;           // Completion header being built
    48                                               sb_opcode_e   cpl_opcode;        // Determined completion opcode
    49                                               logic [63:0]  cpl_payload;       // Data payload (rf_rdata or Original_Header)
    50                                               logic         is_64bit_read;     
    51                                               logic         is_32bit_read;     
    52                                               
    53                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    54                                               // Decode original request opcode & Construct Payload
    55                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    56              1                          3     always_comb begin
    57              1                          3         req_hdr.raw = Original_Header;
    58                                               
    59              1                          3         is_32bit_read = (req_hdr.req.opcode == SB_32_MEM_READ) || (req_hdr.req.opcode == SB_32_CFG_READ);
    60              1                          3         is_64bit_read = (req_hdr.req.opcode == SB_64_MEM_READ) || (req_hdr.req.opcode == SB_64_CFG_READ);
    61                                               
    62                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    63                                                   // Choose completion opcode & Payload (Fixed for UR/CA Fast-Forward)
    64                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    65                                                   if (|status) begin
    66                                                       // [FIX]: Any error (UR/CA) -> Return 64b Data containing Original Header
    67              1                    ***0***             cpl_opcode  = SB_COMPLETION_WITH_64_DATA;
    68              1                    ***0***             cpl_payload = Original_Header;
    69                                                   end else if (is_32bit_read) begin
    70                                                       // Successful 32-bit read
    71              1                          1             cpl_opcode  = SB_COMPLETION_WITH_32_DATA;
    72              1                          1             cpl_payload = {32'h0, rf_rdata[31:0]};  // lower 32 bits only
    73                                                   end else if (is_64bit_read) begin
    74                                                       // Successful 64-bit read
    75              1                    ***0***             cpl_opcode  = SB_COMPLETION_WITH_64_DATA;
    76              1                    ***0***             cpl_payload = rf_rdata;
    77                                                   end else begin
    78                                                       // Write completed successfully – no data
    79              1                          2             cpl_opcode  = SB_COMPLETION_WITHOUT_DATA;
    80              1                          2             cpl_payload = '0;
    81                                                   end
    82                                               
    83                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    84                                                   // Build completion header
    85                                                   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    86              1                          3         cpl_hdr            = '0;
    87              1                          3         cpl_hdr.cpl.opcode = cpl_opcode;
    88                                               
    89                                                   // Mirror Tag
    90              1                          3         cpl_hdr.cpl.tag    = req_hdr.req.tag;  
    91                                               
    92                                                   // Insert Status
    93              1                          3         cpl_hdr.cpl.status = status;
    94                                               
    95                                                   // Swap srcid/dstid reserved for
    96              1                          3         cpl_hdr.cpl.srcid  = sb_srcid_e'(0);   
    97              1                          3         cpl_hdr.cpl.dstid  = sb_dstid_e'(0);   
    98                                               
    99                                                   // Mirror Byte Enables (BE)
    100             1                          3         cpl_hdr.cpl.be     = req_hdr.req.be;
    101                                              
    102                                                  // [FIX]: Data Parity (even Parity -> XOR)
    103                                                  // dp = XOR of payload bits for data-carrying completions; 0 otherwise
    104             1                          3         cpl_hdr.cpl.dp     = (cpl_opcode != SB_COMPLETION_WITHOUT_DATA) ? (^cpl_payload) : 1'b0;
    105                                              
    106                                                  // Control parity placeholder (calculated sequentially to avoid long combinational path)
    107             1                          3         cpl_hdr.cpl.cp     = 1'b0;   
    108                                              end
    109                                              
    110                                              // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    111                                              // Sequential – register output when FSM triggers completion
    112                                              // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    113             1                          5     always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    114                                                  if (!rst_n) begin
    115             1                          2             completion_msg <= '0;
    116             1                          2             completion_vld <= 1'b0;
    117                                                  end else if (completion_start) begin
    118                                                      
    119                                                      // [FIX]: Control Parity (even Parity -> XOR)
    120                                                      // Compute even parity over final header[62:0]
    121             1                    ***0***             automatic logic [62:0] hdr_low = cpl_hdr.raw[62:0];
    122             1                    ***0***             automatic logic cp_final       = (^hdr_low);
    123                                              
    124                                                      // Assemble final 128-bit packet
    125             1                    ***0***             completion_msg <= {cpl_payload, cp_final, cpl_hdr.raw[62:0]};
    126             1                    ***0***             completion_vld <= 1'b1;
    127                                                      
    128                                                  end else if (completion_rdy && completion_vld) begin
    129                                                      // Downstream accepted; de-assert valid
    130             1                    ***0***             completion_vld <= 1'b0;
    131             1                    ***0***             completion_msg <= '0;

    6                                                module sb_pll (
    7                                                    input  logic       en,          // Enable signal
    8                                                    output reg         clk,         // Output PLL clock (800 MHz)
    9                                                    output real        local_period // Output period (ps)
    10                                               );
    11                                                   // =========================
    12                                                   // Initialize clock
    13                                                   // =========================
    14              1                          1         initial clk = 0;
    15                                               
    16                                                   // =========================
    17                                                   // Clock generation process
    18                                                   // =========================
    19                                                   always begin
    20                                                       // If PLL is disabled
    21                                                       if (!en) begin
    22              1                    ***0***                 clk = 0;                // Force clock to 0
    23              1                    ***0***                 @(posedge en);          // Wait until enable becomes 1
    24                                                       end
    25                                                       else begin
    26                                                           // 800 MHz corresponds to a period of 1.25 ns = 1250 ps
    27              1                      12208                 local_period = 1250.0;
    28                                                           // Toggle clock every half period
    29              1                      12208                 #(local_period/2.0) clk = ~clk;
    29              2                      12207

================================Statement Details================================

Statement Coverage for instance /SideBand_Top_tb/dut_inst[0]/dut/u_sb_serializer/forward_clock --

    Line         Item                      Count     Source 
    ----         ----                      -----     ------ 
  File rtl/common/CLK_GATE.sv
    6                                                module CLK_GATE (
    7                                                input      CLK_EN,
    8                                                input      CLK,
    9                                                output     GATED_CLK
    10                                               );
    11                                               
    12                                               `ifdef FPGA
    13                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    14                                               // FPGA target: use the Xilinx BUFGCE global-clock buffer with clock enable.
    15                                               // BUFGCE is a glitch-free clock gate that drives the GLOBAL clock network
    16                                               // (it captures CE internally on the falling edge, matching the low-phase
    17                                               // latch model below). This is the ONLY correct way to gate a clock on FPGA;
    18                                               // a LUT-driven "CLK & latch" net causes glitches and clock-routing warnings.
    19                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    20                                               BUFGCE u_bufgce (
    21                                                   .I  (CLK),
    22                                                   .CE (CLK_EN),
    23                                                   .O  (GATED_CLK)
    24                                               );
    25                                               `else
    26                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    27                                               // Simulation / ASIC model: low-phase latch + AND (integrated clock-gate cell).
    28                                               // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29                                               //internal connections
    30                                               reg     Latch_Out ;
    31                                               
    32                                               //latch (Level Sensitive Device)
    33              1                         80     always @(CLK or CLK_EN)
    34                                                begin
    35                                                 if(!CLK)      // active low
    36                                                  begin
    37              1                         54         Latch_Out <= CLK_EN ;
    38                                                  end
    39                                                end
    40                                               
    41                                               
    42                                               // ANDING
    43              1                      12236     assign  GATED_CLK = CLK & Latch_Out ;
